_purpose: 25 ambitious 3D / 2.5D / raycast games across varied vibes + textures, to grow the catalog and exercise the gravity classifier, the pointer-lock B-catch (BATON.PLSHIM), and the playable-lighting wisdom (tjs-025). Run via clean_batch_driver.py (settle-following, one baton at a time, auto-answers the fork). NO 2D controls (snake/tetris) — these are real games. 'lane' tags the renderer family; 'desiredFork' picks 2.5D vs 3D for the offer-fork wells so we compare/contrast.

entries.goal: Build a cyberpunk neon arena shooter: three distinct enemy types (chaser, ranged, brute), a railgun + a rapid blaster, ammo/reload, health pickups, and a wave counter. Dark-neon palette but the arena floor and enemies must be clearly lit and readable.

Build a medieval castle-siege FPS: defend the gate from waves of invaders climbing ladders. Stone-and-torch vibe, warm daylight so the courtyard is clearly visible. Crossbow with reload, a parapet to run along.

Build a zombie-survival FPS in a concrete bunker: flickering emergency lights but the corridors must stay navigable (bright enough to see). Shotgun, limited ammo, zombies shamble from doorways, a wave/score HUD.
Build a space-station-defense FPS: zero-G corridors with starfield windows, drone enemies, a plasma rifle. Clean sci-fi white-and-cyan lighting so the station reads clearly.
Build a voxel island world you can walk around, mine, and place blocks in, with a bright day sky, grass/dirt/stone/sand block types, and a hotbar. Minecraft-style but cheerful and well-lit.
Build a voxel cave-miner: dig down through layered rock to find glowing ore, carry a lantern that lights the area around you so it is never pitch black. Block break/place, simple inventory.
Build a hover-racer through a neon canyon: boost pads, three laps, a lap timer and best-lap display, banked turns. Smooth 60fps motion (delta-time), bright track edges so the route is obvious.
Build a low-poly kart racer on a sunny grass-and-asphalt circuit: drift, a few AI karts, 3 laps, position + lap HUD. Cheerful daylight, readable track.

Build a relaxing glider that soars over low-poly islands collecting glowing rings against the clock. SMOOTH delta-time flight (no choppiness), gentle banking controls, a clear goal (collect all rings / beat the timer), warm sunset light. Make it feel purposeful and chill.
Build an underwater reef explorer: swim through caves and coral, schools of fish that scatter, light shafts from the surface, a depth/oxygen meter. Bright aqua lighting so the reef is vivid.

Build a low-poly forest diorama you can orbit: a day/night cycle (sun arc + moon), wandering deer, swaying trees, a campfire that glows at night. Lighting must keep the scene readable at all times of the cycle.

Build a marble-roll maze: tilt the board (WASD/arrows) to guide a rolling ball through a 3D labyrinth to the goal, with holes to avoid and a timer. Real physics feel (gravity + rolling), bright top light.
Build a 3D tower-defense: place turrets along a winding path to stop waves of creeps reaching the base, with gold, turret upgrades, and a wave HUD. Bright top-down-ish camera, clearly lit board.

Build a real-3D dungeon crawler: torch-lit stone corridors, skeletons that chase, a sword swing, keys and locked doors. ATMOSPHERIC but PLAYABLE — the torches + an ambient fill must keep corridors clearly visible (not pitch black).

Build a third-person collectathon platformer: a cute character runs/jumps around a floating-island level collecting stars, double-jump, a few moving platforms, a star counter. Bright, colorful, well-lit.

Build a lunar-lander style 3D game: pilot a lander down to pads on a cratered moon surface against gravity and limited fuel, with a thrust/tilt control and a fuel + velocity HUD. Earthrise in the sky, rim-lit terrain.

Build a particle galaxy fly-through: tens of thousands of stars as three.js Points forming a spiral galaxy you can fly into, with nebula color gradients and a slow auto-rotate plus free-fly. Bright, glowing.
Build a doom-style raycaster shooter set in ancient catacombs: bone-and-torch textures, hitscan pistol + shotgun, imps that charge, a health/ammo HUD. Keep corridors lit enough to navigate.
Build a wolfenstein-style raycaster: escape a WW2 bunker, grey-stone + steel-door textures, guards that shoot, a key-card to the exit, a minimap. Bright interior lighting.

Build a raycaster horror crawler in an abandoned asylum: peeling-tile walls, a flashlight cone that reveals the hallway ahead, notes to collect, a slow stalker enemy. Tense but you can SEE where the flashlight points.
Build a first-person raycaster maze-runner through an Aztec temple: gold-and-stone textures, find the exit before the timer, collect idols, pressure-plate traps. Warm torch lighting.

Build an OutRun-style 2.5D pseudo-3D road racer at sunset: a scaling-sprite road that curves and hills, palm trees and signs whipping past, a speedo + timer, traffic to dodge. Vibrant Miami-sunset palette.
Build a Mode-7-style top-down racer: a textured ground plane that rotates/scales under a kart (SNES F-Zero feel), 3 laps, boost strips, a lap timer. Bright, colorful track.
Build an isometric 2.5D city-builder sandbox: place roads, houses, and parks on a grid, a day palette, a simple population counter, drag-to-build. Clean, bright, readable iso tiles.
Build a 2.5D top-down tank arena: drive a tank, rotating turret aims at the mouse, destructible crates, enemy tanks with simple AI, a score/lives HUD. Bright desert-camo palette.
